description_long: This is a mid-rise office building with a symmetrical facade, featuring four stories above ground level. The exterior is clad in light brown brickwork, with large rectangular windows arranged in a regular grid pattern across each floor. The ground floor includes a series of wide windows and a prominent main entrance, while the upper floors have arched windows on the topmost story and rectangular windows below. The roof is steeply pitched and covered in reddish-brown metal, with dormer windows providing additional light to the upper floor. The building's base is finished in a darker material, likely stone or concrete, providing visual contrast and structural stability. Overall, the design is utilitarian with subtle classical influences, suitable for a commercial or business district.
description: A four-story office building with a brick facade, regular window grid, and a steep reddish-brown metal roof with dormers. The ground floor features large windows and a main entrance, while the upper floors have arched and rectangular windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, large ground-floor windows, and symmetrical window arrangement with arched windows on the top floor.
description_view_1: Rear or alternate side view with no visible entrance, regular window grid, and a dark metal roof section.
description_view_2: Opposite facade to the front, with a similar window arrangement but a different ground-floor door placement and no prominent main entrance.
description_view_3: Side or rear view with uniform window grid and dark metal roof, lacking any visible entrance.
attribute_window_pattern: Regularly spaced, vertically aligned rectangular windows on all floors, with arched windows on the top floor; consistent grid pattern.
